data.title: Germany Industrial Production Beats Estimates
data.description:
Industrial production in Germany increased 2% month-over-month in January 2025, rebounding from a 1.5% decline in December 2024 and beating market expectations of a 1.5% increase.

The strongest boost came from the automotive sector (+6.4%), while the food industry (+7.5%) and machine maintenance and assembly (+15.6%) also contributed positively.

On the other hand, output in the manufacture of fabricated metal products contracted by 7.7%, and energy production fell by 0.5%.

The less volatile three-month-on-three-month comparison showed that industrial production stagnated in the period from November 2024 to January 2025.

On a yearly basis, industrial output declined by 1.6%.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Uzbek State Miner Navoi Targets GBP4 Billion London IPO
data.description:
(Updates to add JPMorgan's statement)

Uzbek state miner Navoi Mining & Metallurgical Co. is targeting a London listing in 2025 with a potential valuation of 4 billion pounds sterling, Bloomberg News reported March 7, citing people familiar with the matter.

The gold miner is working with Rothschild & Co. as an adviser for the possible initial public offer, according to the people, who added that Citigroup Inc. and JPMorgan Chase could also be enlisted to work on the IPO.

The considerations are at an early stage and the plans may be delayed or changed, the people cautioned.

JPMorgan declined to comment when reached by MT Newswires. Navoi, Rothschild and Citigroup did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Huaiji Dengyun Auto-parts to Set Up Overseas Factories for 100 Million Yuan
data.description:
Huaiji Dengyun Auto-parts Holding plans to invest up to 100 million yuan to set up overseas factories, including a valve business in Thailand, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

The factories will be set up through the Chinese automotive engine company's subsidiaries in Thailand, Singapore and other markets.

data.title: Deliveroo Hong Kong's Platform to Remain Live Until April 7
Earnings Flash (CKN.L) CLARKSON Reports FY24 EPS GBX286.90
MAS Tightens Digital Payment Regulations
data.description:
The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has banned digital payment token services providers from offering credit or leverage to retail customers and restricted their ability to accept credit cards, according to a parliamentary speech last Wednesday.

MAS has issued a warning over the nature of cryptocurrencies and cautioned that regulatory measures are not enough to protect customers from losses.

data.title: Earnings Flash (CKN.L) CLARKSON Reports FY24 Revenue GBP661.4M
Earnings Flash (ZNWD.L) Zinnwald Lithium Posts FY24 Loss EUR-0.01
Earnings Flash (ORCA.L) Orcadian Energy Reports Fiscal H1 Loss GBX-0.60
Earnings Flash (DATA.L) GLOBALDATA Posts FY24 EPS GBX3.70
Greenwing Resources Plans to Update Polymetallic Resource for the Tasmania Project, Appoints COO
data.description:
Greenwing Resources said it plans to update the polymetallic resource estimate for the Que River project in Tasmania in the coming weeks, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

The project's polymetallic resources inclusive of gold, silver, zinc, lead, and copper were released under an earlier code and will now be updated to achieve code compliance.

It appointed Chris Godfrey as its chief operating officer to oversee the firm's assets.

data.title: Feng Tay Enterprises Board OKs NT$249 Million Land Acquisition
data.description:
Feng Tay Enterprises' board approved the purchase of land from its 10.82% shareholder, Mei-Hui Wang Liu, for NT$249.1 million.

The property spanning 37,200.04 square meters, will be acquired at an agreed price of NT$6,697 per square meter, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The property will be used to expand the shoe manufacturer's development center at the Feng Tay factory in Yunlin County.

data.title: European Markets Head for Higher Open
data.description:
European equity markets were set for a higher open on Monday, recovering losses from the previous session as risk appetite improved, while investors continued to navigate global economic and trade uncertainties.

Last week, the European Central Bank delivered another 25 basis point rate cut, but signaled that monetary policy is becoming less restrictive.

Meanwhile, European leaders agreed to boost defense spending to strengthen regional security amid the ongoing war in Ukraine.

Investors will also assess fresh German trade figures and Italian producer inflation data due on Monday, though there are no major earnings releases scheduled.

Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures advanced 0.7% and 0.5%, respectively, in premarket trade.

data.title: Earnings Flash (DATA.L) GLOBALDATA Reports FY24 Revenue GBP285.5M
Market Chatter: Stellantis Appoints China COO
data.description:
Stellantis appointed Xin Tianshu to succeed Grégoire Olivier as the chief operation officer of its China operations, YiCai Global reported March 7.

Xin previously served as the chief executive of the European automaker's joint venture with Chinese automobile manufacturer Leapmotor, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: RBC Lifts Melrose Industries' EPS Estimates on Positive Outlook; Outperform Rating Affirmed
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets on March 7 raised Melrose Industries' EPS forecast while keeping its rating at outperform on the back of "solid" 2025 guidance and 2029 targets.

For 2025, the aerospace company continues to expect a revenue of 3.55 billion pounds sterling to 3.70 billion pounds. On the other hand, adjusted operating profit is anticipated at the midpoint of 700 million pounds, above the consensus and RBC estimate of 697 million pounds and 687 million pounds, respectively.

By 2029, Melrose targets 5 billion pounds in revenue and adjusted operating profit of 1.2 billion pounds, higher than the consensus estimate and RBC's projection of 1 billion pounds and 971 million pounds, respectively.

Consequently, RBC analysts raised their estimates for the company's adjusted EPS to 0.3356 pound in 2025, 0.4056 pound in 2026, and 0.4955 pound in 2027 from 0.324 pound, 0.3894 pound, and 0.465 pound, respectively. The price target was maintained at 7.6 pounds sterling.

"While the Melrose share has not reacted well to its FY results, we see the update as highly supportive," the research firm said. "The 2025 outlook is supportive of consensus and our EPS rises 4%. And the 2029E targets were ~20% above consensus for EBITA and FCF, with the FCF guide in particular a clear support in our view."

data.title: REG - Hammerson PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 8930Z Hammerson PLC 10 March 2025  

10 March 2025

Hammerson plc (the "Company")

TRANSACTION IN OWN SHARES

The Company announces that, in accordance with the terms of its share buyback programme announced on 16 October 2024 (the "Share Buyback Programme"), the Company has purchased the following number of its ordinary shares of 5 pence each from Morgan Stanley & Co. International plc. Such purchase was effected pursuant to instructions issued by the Company on 16 October 2024.

7 March 2025

Number of ordinary shares purchased:

92,474

Highest price paid per share (pence):

259.40

Lowest price paid per share (pence):

254.20

Volume weighted average price paid per share (pence):

257.47

The Company will cancel the purchased shares.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 as incorporated into and implemented under English law (including by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018), a full breakdown of the individual trades made by Morgan Stanley & Co. International plc on behalf of the Company as part of the Share Buyback Programme is attached to this document and can be accessed through the link provided below.

http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/8930Z_1-2025-3-7.pdf

Since 16 October 2024 pursuant to the Share Buyback Programme, the Company has purchased 10,152,274 ordinary shares.

This announcement does not constitute, or form part of, an offer or any solicitation of an offer for securities in any jurisdiction.

Contact:

Josh Warren

Director of Strategy, Commercial Finance and Investor Relations

Tel: +44 20 7887 1053

josh.warren@hammerson.com

Richard Crowle

Senior Assistant Company Secretary

Tel: +44 20 7887 1000

Richard.crowle@hammerson.com

The announcement above has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.

data.title: REG - Chrysalis Investment - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 8892Z Chrysalis Investments Limited 10 March 2025  

10 March 2025

Chrysalis Investments Limited ("Chrysalis" or the "Company")

Transaction in own shares

The Company announces that, in accordance with the terms of its share buyback programme (the "Programme") announced on 26 September 2024, it has repurchased the following number of its ordinary shares through Panmure Liberum.

Date of Purchase

07 March 2025

Number of ordinary shares purchased

250,000

Weighted average price paid (£)

0.964512

All Ordinary Shares purchased will be held in treasury. Following the above purchase, the Company holds 49,083,261 Ordinary Shares in treasury.

The total number of Ordinary Shares in issue excluding shares held as treasury shares is 546,067,153. 

The above figure of 546,067,153 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

-ENDS-

For further information, please contact

Media

Montfort Communications:

Charlotte McMullen / Imogen Saunders

+44 (0) 7921 881 800

chrysalis@montfort.london

Investment Adviser

Chrysalis Investment Partners LLP:

James Simpson

+44 (0) 20 7871 5343

G10 Capital Limited (AIFM):

+44 (0) 20 7397 5450

Maria Baldwin

Panmure Liberum:

Chris Clarke / Darren Vickers

+44 (0) 20 3100 2222

Deutsche Numis:

Nathan Brown / Matt Goss

+44 (0) 20 7260 1000

IQEQ Fund Services (Guernsey) Limited:

Aimee Gontier / Elaine Smeja

+44 (0) 1481 231852

LEI: 213800F9SQ753JQHSW24

A copy of this announcement will be available on the Company's website at https://www.chrysalisinvestments.co.uk

The information contained in this announcement regarding the Company's investments has been provided by the relevant underlying portfolio company and has not been independently verified by the Company. The information contained herein is unaudited.

This announcement is for information purposes only and is not an offer to invest. All investments are subject to risk. Past performance is no guarantee of future returns. Prospective investors are advised to seek expert legal, financial, tax and other professional advice before making any investment decision. The value of investments may fluctuate. Results achieved in the past are no guarantee of future results. Neither the content of the Company's website, nor the content on any website accessible from hyperlinks on its website for any other website, is incorporated into, or forms part of, this announcement nor, unless previously published by means of a recognised information service, should any such content be relied upon in reaching a decision as to whether or not to acquire, continue to hold, or dispose of, securities in the Company.

The Company is an alternative investment fund ("AIF") for the purposes of the AIFM Directive and as such is required to have an investment manager who is duly authorised to undertake the role of an alternative investment fund manager ("AIFM"). The AIFM appointed is G10 Capital Limited (part of the IQEQ Group).

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. data.title: REG - Caledonia Investmnts - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 8932Z Caledonia Investments PLC 10 March 2025  

Caledonia Investments plc

Transaction in own shares

Caledonia Investments plc ("Caledonia") announces that on 7 March 2025 it purchased 5,867 of its own ordinary shares of 5 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") through J.P. Morgan Securities plc at an average price of 3879.56 pence per share. The purchased Ordinary Shares will be cancelled.

Number of ordinary shares purchased:

5,867

Lowest price per share paid (pence):

3870.00

Highest price per share paid (pence):

3880.00

Weighted average price paid (pence):

3879.56

Following the above transaction, Caledonia's issued share capital includes 53,028,493 Ordinary Shares with voting rights.

For further information, please contact:

Verity Cox

Deputy Company Secretary

Caledonia Investments plc              

Telephone +44 20 7802 8080

10 March 2025

END

data.title: REG - Barratt Redrow PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 8894Z Barratt Redrow PLC 10 March 2025  

10 March 2025

Barratt Redrow plc

Transactions in own shares

Barratt Redrow plc, ISIN: GB0000811801 (the "Company", "Barratt Redrow") announces that on 07 March 2025 it purchased for cancellation the following number of ordinary shares of 10 pence each from UBS AG London Branch ("UBS") as part of its £50 million share repurchase programme announced on 12 February 2025 (the "Programme"). All shares were purchased from UBS as an "on exchange" transaction subject to the rules of the London Stock Exchange.

Date of purchases:

07 March 2025

Total number of shares purchased:

125,000

Highest price paid per share (pence):

£428.8000

Lowest price paid per share (pence):

£414.4000

Volume weighted average price paid per share (pence):

£421.3606

To date, Barratt Redrow has purchased 2,075,000 ordinary shares in aggregate for cancellation from UBS in accordance with the Programme. Following the above purchases and cancellation of the ordinary shares, the Company holds no ordinary shares in treasury and has 1,449,020,916 ordinary shares in issue (excluding treasury shares). The total number of voting rights in the Company is therefore 1,449,020,916.

A schedule of individual trades carried out by UBS as principal in connection with the above purchase is set out below.
